leads.sector: White Goods & Electricals (CSV bucket: Electronics & Semiconductors)
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — consistently profitable globally; Indian subsidiary LGEIL is among LG's most profitable units and drew a 54x-subscribed IPO

evidence: Listed LG Electronics India Limited (LGEIL) on the NSE on 14 Oct 2025 — IPO of ~INR 11,607 cr (~USD 1.31 bn), 54x subscribed; stock closed +48% on debut
CEO William Cho: 'India is now at the center of our Global South strategy'
Three-part India vision disclosed at listing: 'Make for India', 'Make in India', 'Make India Global'

'Investing INR 50.01 billion in a new factory in Sri City, expected to create 1,900 direct and indirect jobs' — third India plant (USD ~600 mn, AC production from late 2026, phased expansion into refrigerators/washing machines/compressors through 2029; combined capacity by 2029: 2 mn TVs, 3.6 mn refrigerators, 3.75 mn washing machines, 4.7 mn ACs)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Two operating plants (Greater Noida, Pune) + third under construction at Sri City, Andhra Pradesh (247 acres approved); #1 offline-channel player in Indian major home appliances; Indian arm now separately NSE-listed
india_apac_interest.report_used: LG Global newsroom disclosure 'LG Electronics India Lists on NSE' (lg.com, Oct 2025) + LGEIL IPO filing coverage

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — one of the world's most profitable automakers by absolute operating profit in 2024-2025

evidence: Listed Hyundai Motor India Ltd in Oct 2024 in a ~Rs 27,870 cr offering the company itself describes as 'the largest in India's history'
Executive Chair Euisun Chung: 'We knew that India was the future. That is why we continuously increased investments, expanded our R&D capabilities and created more than 250,000 jobs'
Disclosed plan to 'localize the electric vehicle (EV) supply network for battery systems, battery cells and drive systems, and will invest in building EV infrastructure across India'
Rs 32,000 cr India expansion plan (Rs 26,000 cr Chennai, Rs 6,000 cr Pune/Talegaon plant acquired from GM for Rs 787 cr); capacity to rise from ~824k to 1.1 mn units by 2028
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Two Chennai plants + Talegaon (Pune) plant ramping from H2 2025; Indian subsidiary separately listed on NSE/BSE since Oct 2024
india_apac_interest.report_used: Hyundai Motor worldwide newsroom disclosure on the HMIL IPO (hyundai.com) + HMIL IPO/expansion coverage

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — record operating margins among volume automakers in 2024-2025

evidence: Anantapur (Andhra Pradesh) plant capacity expanded from 300,000 to ~350,000 units/yr; India accounts for ~9% of Kia's global sales
Committed Rs 2,000 cr for R&D, infrastructure and EV manufacturing in India; Anantapur plant is 'future proof to produce both EVs and IC engine models together'
Began local EV production at Anantapur in 2025 (Carens Clavis EV) to mitigate import duties; plans EV exports from India
Crossed 2.5 lakh cumulative export units from Anantapur (Seltos largest contributor)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Kia India Pvt Ltd, Anantapur — full manufacturing hub, part of the Hyundai Motor Group Anantapur/Chennai ecosystem with Mobis module plants co-located
india_apac_interest.report_used: Kia India press/industry disclosures (Autocar India, CarDekho); Kia Corp business-report PDF not directly parsed

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — steady mid-single-digit operating margins; large global Tier-1

evidence: Official press release (8 Dec 2025): 'it has established a specialized software research branch in Bengaluru, India's IT hub... alongside its existing integrated R&D center in Hyderabad, established earlier this year, pursuing a dual R&D strategy'

'The company produces modules and core components at its two production hubs in Chennai and Anantapur, while its Gurugram parts hub ensures stable supply of after-sales service parts... Delhi and Chennai host the quality center and procurement center'
'It has set an aggressive target to increase the order value from Indian customers by approximately six times by 2028 compared to 2025'
Bengaluru branch to be a 'specialized hub for infotainment software' citing India's shift to mid-to-large vehicles and rising ADAS adoption
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Two module/component plants (Chennai, Anantapur), Gurugram after-sales parts hub, Hyderabad integrated R&D center, Bengaluru SW branch, Delhi quality center, Chennai procurement center
india_apac_interest.report_used: Hyundai Mobis official newsroom press release (mobis.com, 2025-12-08), fetched and quoted directly

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — positive operating profit through the 2024-2025 steel downcycle, though margins compressed

evidence: Oct 2024 MoU then Jul 2025 Heads of Agreement with JSW Group; JV agreement for a 50:50 co-managed integrated steel mill: 'The joint venture is structured as an equal partnership, with each company holding a 50% stake' with 'an integrated production capacity of 6 million tons of crude steel'

'A site has been secured in Odisha, offering proximity to iron ore mines and efficient access to logistics, power, and infrastructure'; targets 'completion by 2031, following a 48-month construction period'
POSCO's stated rationale: India 'steel consumption has grown by over 10% annually in recent years'; partnership also covers battery materials and renewable energy
History: the original standalone POSCO-India 12 MTPA Odisha project (2005 MoU) stalled on land/permits and was abandoned by 2017 — the JSW JV is the structural fix (local partner) for that failure
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Existing downstream base: POSCO Maharashtra (cold-rolled/galvanized, Talegaon) and steel processing/service centers; new 6 MTPA integrated JV mill in Odisha targeted 2031
india_apac_interest.report_used: Official POSCO Group Newsroom release 'POSCO to Build Integrated Steel Mill in India via Joint Venture' (newsroom.posco.com), fetched and quoted directly + JSW Group press release

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — record revenue/operating profit in 2024-2025 on global K9/Chunmoo export backlog

evidence: Signed second India K9 contract on 3 Apr 2025: ~USD 253-254 mn for 100 additional K9 Vajra-T self-propelled howitzers for the Indian Army (Hanwha Aerospace newsroom: 'Hanwha Aerospace Secures Second K9 Vajra-T Contract from India', 2025-04-03)
Systems manufactured in India by Larsen & Toubro at its Armored Systems Complex, Hazira, Gujarat; localization to rise from ~50% (first batch) to ~60%
First contract (2017, KRW 770 bn) delivered 100 units by 2020 — a decade-long India franchise, not a one-off
India and South Korea have discussed jointly exporting the K9 Vajra-T to third countries
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No owned plant; deep licensed-production partnership with L&T (Hazira) for K9 Vajra-T, plus component supply from Korea to L&T
india_apac_interest.report_used: Hanwha Aerospace English newsroom item seq=499 (headline confirmed on-site; article body did not render) + KED Global / Breaking Defense / Janes coverage

leads.sector: Green Energy & Fuels / Batteries (CSV bucket: White Goods & Electricals)
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE (headline) — positive operating profit in 2024-2025 but heavily dependent on US IRA (AMPC) tax credits; roughly breakeven excluding them

evidence: Signed a non-binding agreement with JSW Energy for a 50:50 India battery JV — >USD 1.5 bn, ~10 GWh plant for EV and renewable-storage cells; JSW to take ~70% of output; production targeted end-2026 (Reuters/Business Standard, Dec 2024)
JSW proposed a 25-year agreement to LGES
LGES's own statement was cautious: 'no specific plans for India have been finalised' — no confirmed public announcement of a binding JV as of this check
Historical India footprint: supplied cells for Tata/Mahindra EV programs and e-scooter makers (Ola) from Korea/China — sales exposure without local manufacturing
india_apac_interest.india_operations: None owned; prospective JSW Energy JV (non-binding as last publicly disclosed); India currently served via cell exports
india_apac_interest.report_used: Reuters-sourced coverage (Business Standard, just-auto, electrive, Dec 2024); no binding-JV confirmation found in later public disclosures — status UNVERIFIED beyond the non-binding agreement

leads.profitability_check: LOSS-MAKING in FY2025 — revenue fell ~20% to KRW 13.27 tn; Energy Solutions segment swung to a KRW 1.85 tn operating loss on the EV demand chasm; no cash dividends for 3 years announced

evidence: Company guidance/IR commentary: expects the small-battery market to grow with the electric two-wheeler market 'centered in India' and 'plans to enter electric two-wheeler battery markets in India and other Southeast Asian countries'
Industry coverage notes Samsung SDI 'is scouting opportunities in India's battery value chain' alongside LGES and CATL
All disclosed 2025 capex commitments are US (GM JV Indiana, USD 3.5 bn; 30 GWh US BESS by 2026) and Hungary — no India investment committed
india_apac_interest.india_operations: None; cylindrical-cell sales to Indian e-mobility customers from overseas plants only
india_apac_interest.report_used: Samsung SDI IR news releases (samsungsdi.com Q1/Q3 2025 earnings, '2025 Samsung SDI in Brief') + FY2025 results coverage; full business report not parsed

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — revenue and operating profit growing on AI-server MLCC/FCBGA demand; ~25% global MLCC share, >40% in AI-server MLCCs

evidence: Only India presence found in company's own global-network disclosure: 'Samsung Electro-Mechanics Software India Bangalore Pvt. Ltd (SEMB)' — a software/R&D office, plus a Bangalore Innovation Centre (LinkedIn)
No India manufacturing plans in any 2025-2026 disclosure; capacity 'is being allocated first to AI server products' at Korean/Philippine/Chinese plants
Indirect India exposure via component supply into Samsung Electronics' Noida smartphone plant supply chain (not separately disclosed)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Bangalore software subsidiary (SEMB) and innovation centre only; no manufacturing
india_apac_interest.report_used: samsungsem.com global-network and newsroom pages (2025 Q4 business performance release); no India section in disclosures

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — consistently profitable on Apple camera-module franchise; diversifying into FC-BGA/automotive sensing

evidence: Company/GlobalData profile lists operations in 'South Korea, Indonesia, China, Vietnam, Mexico, Poland, the US, Japan, Taiwan, and Germany' — India absent

APAC capacity strategy is explicitly Vietnam-centric: new Haiphong camera-module factory with Vietnam targeted at '70% to 80% of its total camera module production output in the next two to three years' (The Elec)
2024-era press speculation about an India camera-module line was not confirmed in any subsequent company disclosure found
india_apac_interest.report_used: LG Innotek IR/newsroom pages + The Elec / DigiTimes coverage; annual report PDF not parsed — India absence corroborated across three sources

leads.profitability_check: MARGINAL — petrochemical downcycle plus battery-materials softness compressed 2024-2025 operating profit; remains investment-grade, cash-generative via LGES stake

evidence: Company disclosure lists production bases in 'Korea, China, Taiwan, Vietnam, India, Poland, and the U.S.' — the India base is LG Polymers India (Visakhapatnam, polystyrene)
Legacy liability rather than growth story: the 7 May 2020 styrene gas leak at LG Polymers' Gopalapatnam plant killed 12 and hospitalized 300+; no India expansion announced since
Cathode/battery-materials capex (80k→260k tons by 2026) is directed at Korea (Gumi), US (Tennessee) and Morocco — not India
india_apac_interest.india_operations: LG Polymers India Pvt Ltd, Visakhapatnam (polystyrene compounding); no battery-materials footprint in India
india_apac_interest.report_used: LG Chem corporate site/MarkLines profile + Wikipedia-corroborated incident record; business report PDF not parsed

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — overseas food revenue ~KRW 5.58 tn (near 50% of food sales), Q4 net income jumped; bio segment cyclical but positive

evidence: Disclosed K-food capacity expansion map is Japan (new bibigo dumpling plant, Chiba, 2025), Hungary (by 2026) and South Dakota, US (by 2027) — India does not appear

APAC strategy runs through Vietnam (major production base), Japan, China, and new Thailand marketing pushes; bibigo sold in '70+ countries' but no India entity, plant, or entry plan found in any company disclosure
Sept 2025 ITOCHU business partnership covers Japan food sectors, again ex-India
india_apac_interest.india_operations: None found (note: CJ Darcl Logistics in India belongs to CJ Logistics, a sibling CJ Group affiliate, not CJ Cheiljedang)
india_apac_interest.report_used: cj.co.kr investor pages, Korea Herald / Herald Business coverage of overseas expansion; annual report PDF not parsed

method_notes: All 12 companies verified present in candidates_kr.csv by ticker: A066570, A005380, A000270, A012330, A005490, A012450, A373220, A006400, A009150, A011070, A051910, A097950; sector field reflects the CSV FocusSector bucket where it differs from the natural sector

Requested shipbuilders (HD Hyundai Heavy Industries, HD KSOE, Samsung Heavy Industries, Hanwha Ocean) are NOT in the candidate CSV — its 'Shipbuilding & Marine' bucket (11 names) contains only shipping/logistics companies (HMM, Pan Ocean, Korea Line, etc.), so no shipbuilding lead is included per the verify-before-including rule

Evidence grounded in ~16 web searches/fetches: direct quotes pulled from official newsrooms of Hyundai Mobis (mobis.com press idx=6153, via curl with Mozilla UA), POSCO (newsroom.posco.com), LG (lg.com global newsroom), Hyundai Motor (hyundai.com worldwide newsroom); remainder from IR-sourced wire coverage (KED Global, Business Standard, Reuters-syndicated)

DART (dart.fss.or.kr) API not used — requires key registration, which was explicitly out of scope; formal audited annual-report PDFs were not parsed, so 'report_used' cites the nearest official disclosure (newsroom/IR release or IPO filing coverage)

Profitability checks are FY2024-FY2025 assessments from earnings releases/coverage, not recomputed from statements; Samsung SDI is the one loss-maker included deliberately (battery-sector leader with an explicitly stated India e2W entry intent)
Korean-language sources avoided per instruction (인도 homonym risk); all evidence is from English disclosures
site_access_issues: morningstar.com returned HTTP 403 on the Hyundai Mobis PR — worked around by fetching the identical release from mobis.com directly

m.hanwhaaerospace.com newsroom page rendered only the headline ('Hanwha Aerospace Secures Second K9 Vajra-T Contract from India', 2025-04-03) — article body is JS-loaded; contract details ($253-254 mn, 100 units, L&T Hazira, 60% localization) therefore corroborated from KED Global / Breaking Defense / Janes instead

LG Energy Solution: no official IR page confirming the JSW JV was found — only the Dec-2024 non-binding agreement is publicly evidenced, and LGES itself said 'no specific plans for India have been finalised'; final JV status marked UNVERIFIED
CJ Cheiljedang and LG Innotek India absence is an argument from silence across multiple sources, not a company statement
dart.fss.or.kr not queried (API key registration required, prohibited by task)
